---
title: "TITLE HERE"
subtitle: "Note 00X — one-line hook that makes someone want to read on."
description: "The one-sentence promise: exactly what the reader walks away knowing."
date: YYYY-MM-DD
categories: [AI]
author: "Mark Cribb"
draft: true
---

<!--
  HOW THIS WORKS
  - One idea per note. ~600-900 words. ~3-minute read.
  - `draft: true` keeps it off the live site until you remove this line.
  - Generate a fresh copy with:  ./New-CribbNote.ps1 -Title "Your Title"
-->

Open with the question, the moment, or the misconception that made this worth writing.

## The one idea

State it plainly. The reader should be able to repeat it in a sentence afterward.

## Why it matters

Connect it to a decision someone actually makes — a meeting, a budget, a bet.

## Show the gears

The part that turns "magic" into "oh, that's all it is." A small example, a diagram,
or a link to a runnable [demo](demos.qmd).

*Feels like magic. It's math.*

— Mark
